screens.name: projects/10997952019249158992/screens/337260763fc74b29937e3bdd47d109bc
screens.prompt:
Design a high-fidelity, Stripe-inspired landing page for Clicks Protocol. 
Background: #0F0814 (dark purple). Accent: #00FF9B (neon green). Surface: #191020 (glassmorphic cards). Text: white. Font: Inter.
Sections:
1) Hero: Headline "Autonomous Yield for AI Agents". Subtext: "The first DeFi yield protocol built for AI agents on Base L2. Smart 80/20 USDC split earning 4-8% APY with zero manual management." Two CTAs: "Launch App" (neon green) and "Read Docs" (ghost). Include a realistic code snippet component showing a sample SDK call on the right.
2) Stats: Horizontal bar with four metrics: "80/20 Split", "4-8% APY", "0 Lockup", "1 SDK Call".
3) How it Works: Three steps: (1) Deposit USDC, (2) Auto-allocation, (3) Accrue Yield. Each with a modern icon.
4) Developers: Two cards using glassmorphism. Card 1: "Clicks SDK - Integrate yield into any AI agent with one line of code." Card 2: "MCP Server - Native support for Model Context Protocol agents."
5) Footer: Minimal with "Subscribe to Updates" email input and neon green "Join" button.
Aesthetic: Modern, clean, enterprise SaaS, developer-centric, use glassmorphic effects for cards. Dark mode.
screens.title: Clicks Protocol Landing Page

outputComponents.text:
I've designed the full landing page for Clicks Protocol as you described. 

It features:
*   **A High-Impact Hero:** Leading with the "Autonomous Yield for AI Agents" headline and a functional-looking code snippet to immediately signal the developer focus.
*   **A Unified Stats Bar:** Showcasing the 80/20 split and 4-8% APY metrics.
*   **"How it Works" and Developer Cards:** Utilizing glassmorphic surface cards to keep the design modern and professional, similar to high-end SaaS platforms.
*   **Branding:** Applied your #00FF9B neon green as the primary accent on the deep #0F0814 purple background.

How does this look for the initial concept? Let me know if you'd like to tweak any sections or add more detail to the developer documentation area!

outputComponents.suggestion: Flesh out the SDK documentation page
